title: Oil Wavers as Persian Gulf Flows Surge Toward Pre-War Levels
summary: Oil prices wavered in thin trading after their biggest quarterly slump since 2020, as flows from the Persian Gulf recovered toward pre-war levels and the US and Iran continued talks on a permanent peace deal.

title: Stocks Climb in US Holiday Trading, Dollar Falls: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks rose as the latest bout of jitters about the sustainability of the AI-trade subsided, with Europe’s benchmark holding near a record high. The dollar was on track for its worst week since May.

title: US Stocks Post Largest Outflows in Over Three Months, BofA Says
summary: Investors are turning away from US stocks at the fastest pace since March, according to Bank of America Corp.

title: HSBC, Standard Chartered Weigh SRTs as Asia-Linked Deals Ramp Up
summary: HSBC Holdings Plc is laying the groundwork for a significant risk transfer linked to a portfolio of Asia-Pacific loans as it ramps up use of the hedging instrument.

title: Taiwanese Brokerage Yuanta Taps Banks for $1.3 Billion of Loans
summary: Yuanta Securities Co., Taiwan’s largest brokerage, and its subsidiary, are seeking up to NT$42 billion ($1.3 billion) through two syndicated loans, according to people familiar with the matter, a fundraising that highlights surging demand for financial services amid the stock boom.

title: Food Prices Ease in June as Focus Shifts From War to Crops
summary: Global food prices edged lower as a ceasefire between the US and Iran reduced concerns of further disruptions to supply chains.

title: Won Jumps as Korea Prepares for Currency Flows From Hynix ADRs
summary: South Korea’s won strengthened after a person familiar with the matter said the nation’s officials were preparing for currency flows related to SK Hynix Inc.’s offering of American depositary receipts.

title: How a Major Grocery Store Chain Can Dramatically Lower the Cost of Food
summary: In June, grocery giant Aldi opened a store just off of Times Square in Manhattan. It's the company's first location in Midtown and, according to their Chief Commercial Officer Scott Patton, Aldi has to orchestrate a "logistical symphony" to get groceries into the middle of one of the busiest places in America. For instance, they use shorter trucks to navigate the tight corners of New York City streets. On this episode, we speak with Patton about what it took to open this specific Aldi and why they chose a busy tourist location like Times Square. He also explains how the company — famous for its low prices — is able to sell even wagyu ground beef at a consumer-friendly price point, how the mostly private-label grocer thinks through which name brands to incorporate into their stock, Aldi's cult-favorite "Aisle of Shame," a short history of barcode innovation, and how GLP-1s are changing consumer habits. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Japan’s 2026 Wage Talks End in Third Year of Gains Above 5%
summary: Japan’s annual wage negotiations concluded with average pay gains topping 5% for a third year, underscoring the economy’s resilience and reinforcing the Bank of Japan’s stance to keep raising interest rates.

title: The Race to Rescue 8,000 Sailors Still Stranded Behind Hormuz
summary: After more than 100 days trapped in the Persian Gulf, thousands of seafarers are still waiting for a chance to sail out of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Euro-Zone Business Activity Revised Up to Stagnation for June
summary: Business activity in the euro area was better than initially thought last month, with the bloc recording stagnation rather than shrinking.

title: China Urges ‘Unimpeded Passage’ of Hormuz as Fee Chatter Mounts
summary: China called for the unhindered flow of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, comments that come as leading European powers are apparently accepting that vessels will have to pay fees to Iran and Oman.

title: Total Offers Iraqi Oil to Asian Refiners as Supply Swells
summary: French oil major TotalEnergies SE is offering millions of barrels of Iraqi crude that can be promptly delivered to Asia, according to traders, adding to a market already brimming with supply.

title: Lagarde Won’t Exclude Early ECB Exit to Enter French Politics
summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said she won’t rule out leaving her current position early to participate in French politics.

title: France’s Lescure Wants 2027 Budget That Brings Deficit Below 5%
summary: French Finance Minister Roland Lescure said he will push for a finance bill for next year that brings a further improvement in public finances, and expects parliament “most likely” will allow a budget to pass.

title: Yen Isn't Behaving Like a G10 Currency, Mizuho Says
summary: Jordan Rochester, Mizuho FICC strategy EMEA head, discusses sharp moves in the yen. "From all the frameworks, dollar-yen should be lower," he tells Bloomberg Television. He adds there has been a change in behaviors in the Japanese space and the yen is not behaving like a G10 currency. "It trades like an EM in terms of its correlation to rates," Rochester says.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Qatar LNG Ship Traffic Through Hormuz Resumes After Brief Pause
summary: Qatar appears to be sending a liquefied natural gas tanker through the Strait of Hormuz, the first visible passage in about a week, as the major exporter eyes a rapid restart to output.

title: Citi Says Oil May Slump to $60 as Hormuz Shock Fades Away
summary: Brent oil could extend declines to $60 a barrel by year-end as disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz ease, according to Citigroup Inc., adding to a chorus of bearish outlooks for the global crude market.

title: South Korean Stocks Jump 6% After Turbulent Week on AI Swings
summary: South Korean stocks rebounded after a near 10% drop in the previous two sessions, highlighting skittish investor sentiment as scrutiny grows over the sustainability of the global AI boom.

title: Renk Group Nears Deal to Buy UK’s David Brown Defence
summary: Industrial machinery maker Renk Group AG has agreed a deal to acquire privately held David Brown Defence, in a move that gives it more exposure to the naval sector.

title: Yen Strengthens as Traders Brace for Holiday Intervention Risk
summary: Options traders are paying up to hedge against sharp moves in the yen ahead of thin US holiday trading, as speculation grows that Japanese authorities may be less predictable in how they intervene to support the currency.

title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 7/3/2026
summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Rises Toward $4,200 as Weak Jobs Data Lowers Rate-Hike Odds
summary: Gold advanced for a third day after weak US jobs data reduced the chances of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates this year to tackle inflation.

title: Traders Weigh Second Half Outlook: Markets Snapshot
summary:
The first half of 2026 saw a bumper earnings season and global equity benchmarks soar to new highs, driven by a enthusiasm around the AI trade. Businesses, investors and central banks now turn their attention to the second half of the year with the focus firmly on whether markets can move past rocky geopolitical issues, growth concerns and ongoing jitters around the AI buildout. The Opening Trade spoke to leading voices about how the rest of 2026 is shaping up for equity markets.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Factories Create Winners and Losers in Power Equipment Market
summary: Next-generation AI factories are forcing power equipment firms to rethink their portfolios in the race to profit from a market expected to be worth more than $200 billion a year.
